children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: There is a “Vessel Operations Revolving Fund” for use by the Secretary of Transportation in carrying out duties and powers related to vessel operations, including charter, operation, maintenance, repair, reconditioning, and improvement of merchant vessels under the jurisdiction of the Secretary. The Fund has a working capital of $20,000,000, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—Relationship to Other Laws
children.label: b .—Relationship to Other Laws
children.text: Notwithstanding any other law, rates for shipping services provided under the Fund shall be prescribed by the Secretary and the Fund shall be credited with receipts from vessel operations conducted under the Fund. Sections 1(a) and (c), 3(c), and 4 of the Act of (, (c), 4703(c), 4704), apply to those operations and to seamen employed through general agents as employees of the United States Government. Notwithstanding any other law on the employment of persons by the Government, the seamen may be employed in accordance with customary commercial practices in the maritime industry.March 24, 194350 U.S.C. 4701(a)

children.heading: .—Advancements
children.label: c .—Advancements
children.text: With the approval of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, the Secretary may advance amounts the Secretary considers necessary, but not more than 2 percent of vessel operating expenses, from the Fund to the appropriation “Salaries and Expenses” in carrying out duties and powers related to vessel operations, without regard to the limitations on amounts stated in that appropriation.

children.heading: .—Transfers
children.label: d .—Transfers
children.text: The unexpended balances of working funds or of allocation accounts established after , for the activities provided for in subsection (a), and receipts received from those activities, may be transferred to the Fund, which shall be available for the purposes of those working funds or allocation accounts.January 1, 1951

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Amounts made available to the Secretary for maritime activities by this section or any other law may not be used to pay for a vessel described in paragraph (2) unless the compensation to be paid is computed under as that section is interpreted by the Comptroller General.section 56303 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the title to which is acquired by the Government by requisition or purchase;

children.label: B
children.text: the use of which is taken by requisition or agreement; or

children.label: C
children.text: lost while insured by the Government.

children.heading: .—Nonapplicable vessels
children.label: 3 .—Nonapplicable vessels
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to a vessel under a construction-differential subsidy contract.

necessary expenses incurred in the protection, preservation, maintenance, acquisition, or use of vessels involved in mortgage foreclosure or forfeiture proceedings instituted by the Government, including payment of prior claims and liens, expenses of sale, or other related charges;
necessary expenses incident to the redelivery and lay-up, in the United States, of vessels chartered as of , under agreements not calling for their return to the Government;June 20, 1956
the activation, repair, and deactivation of merchant vessels chartered for limited emergency purposes during fiscal year 1957 under the jurisdiction of the Secretary; and
payment of expenses of custody and maintenance of Government-owned vessels not in the National Defense Reserve Fleet.

children.heading: .—Expenses and Receipts Related to Charter Operations
children.label: g .—Expenses and Receipts Related to Charter Operations
children.text: The Fund is available for expenses incurred in activating, repairing, and deactivating merchant vessels chartered under the jurisdiction of the Secretary. Receipts from charter operations of Government-owned vessels under the jurisdiction of the Secretary shall be credited to the Fund.
